In soccer terminology, a give and go is a move where a player passes the ball to a teammate (the "give") and then immediately runs toward the teammate to receive the ball back (the "go"). It's a quick exchange of passes between two players to advance the ball or create space. This maneuver is commonly used in soccer to evade defenders and keep possession.
